jokes.text: If I'm reading this correctly, we're underwater. You're not reading it correctly.
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor incompetence paired with Leela's no-nonsense response - well-structured exchange

jokes.text: Stick to fish flakes, you overgrown sea monkey!
jokes.punchline: Leela's species-specific insult
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Leela being harsh to Zoidberg with species-appropriate insult

jokes.text: Is that Crab Rangoon? No, he's Dr. Zoidberg.
jokes.punchline: Server treating Zoidberg as food item
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect economy - sets up food expectation, delivers character confusion

jokes.text: Here. Place my lead-lined overcape in the Closet of Heavy Things.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific storage location name
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist worldbuilding - 'Closet of Heavy Things' is perfectly alien and specific

jokes.text: I love my job.
jokes.setup: Being ordered around by demanding alien
jokes.punchline: Deadpan expression of job satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic beat - works as a breather moment but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Wrong! Ndnd got them from an inspirational placemat.
jokes.punchline: Vows actually from restaurant placemat
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - sets up romantic personal vows, reveals mundane commercial source

jokes.text: It was a quality placemat!
jokes.setup: Being called out for plagiarizing vows
jokes.punchline: Defensive about the placemat's quality rather than the plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character response - misses the point of criticism, focuses on wrong detail

jokes.text: your dress covered in the blood of three B'dnkian warriors. You took me in your arms and ate the morsel of B'dnkian entrail lodged in my cleavage. It was delicious.
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely violent and cannibalistic romantic memory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic tropes - alien courtship is viscerally disturbing but treated as romantic

jokes.text: Hey, you got any, B'dnkian entrail on that tray? No, but we have lettuce-wrapped cucumbers, which are even grosser.
jokes.setup: Guest asking for mentioned alien entrails
jokes.punchline: Vegetables described as grosser than alien entrails
jokes.explanation: Great value inversion - healthy food considered more disgusting than alien gore

jokes.text: If you want me to not eat, then you should have done the cooking! Snap!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sassy comeback with finger snap
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg showing unexpected sass, the 'Snap!' sells it

jokes.text: If it please the Lord? Yeah, I love that kinda stuff.
jokes.setup: Formal request for skull crushing ceremony
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's casual enthusiasm for violence
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Lrrr's matter-of-fact enjoyment of brutal customs

jokes.text: Why do I have to use the hook? I hate the hook. You managed to capture me with it at our first wedding. You weren't the intended target.
jokes.punchline: Reveals their marriage began as mistaken identity/accident
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark relationship humor - their entire marriage built on a hunting mistake

jokes.text: But enough about me. Stick to the script.
jokes.setup: Lrrr listing his own positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Abrupt pivot after realizing he's praising himself instead of the groom
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up best man speech, reveals it's just self-praise

jokes.text: Nobody invited the help. Nobody invited me to kick your ass either, yet here we are. Hi-ya!
jokes.setup: Snobbish comment about servers at bouquet toss
jokes.punchline: Amy's combative response and immediate violence
jokes.explanation: Great comeback line with perfect Amy attitude - escalates from verbal to physical instantly

jokes.text: I wonder what I should bake next. Hey, Fry, you ever wanted to be in a pie?
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion of cooking Fry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - casual cannibalistic suggestion delivered as friendly offer

jokes.text: Anything for you, buddy. Point me to the crust, and I'll be there.
jokes.setup: Bender's suggestion to bake Fry into a pie
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic agreement to be cooked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response - completely misses the danger, focuses on being helpful

jokes.text: Scrub-a-dub-dub!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's childish enthusiasm for being cleaned
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat - Zoidberg's childlike excitement is appropriately pathetic

jokes.text: I have the best life ever. That's our Fry. It sure is. It most definitely is.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing contentment with menial labor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward agreement from crew suggesting something's off
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's mystery - the awkward repetition suggests something suspicious

jokes.text: Extremely easily.
jokes.setup: Fry asking how they'll manage without their 'ace delivery boy'
jokes.punchline: Professor's blunt assessment of Fry's replaceability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor brutality - maximum insult with minimum words

jokes.text: Come on. Let's get a temp dimwit to replace our permanent one.
jokes.punchline: Leela categorizing both temp and permanent Fry as dimwits
jokes.explanation: Good structure contrasting temp vs permanent dimwits - harsh but economical

jokes.text: Is that him? Maybe?
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about whether visible person is the manager
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about office dysfunction - suggests chaos in temp agency

jokes.text: Secretary, jockey, lion tamer, bowling shoe spray technician.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal to absurd job types
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to oddly specific - bowling shoe spray technician is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Well, let's see. It's a thankless job. He'll never be thanked. Any skills? Absolutely not! I'll need to berate him for being incompetent!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly brutal honesty about terrible working conditions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Leela's matter-of-fact description of abuse becomes increasingly dark

jokes.text: Then, I've got just the schlep for you.
jokes.punchline: Manager's enthusiastic match-making with perfect victim
jokes.explanation: Good setup payoff - manager's professional enthusiasm for abuse is appropriately cynical

jokes.text: Perfect! He sounds very berate-able.
jokes.punchline: Leela coining 'berate-able' as positive job qualification
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - 'berate-able' as job skill reveals Leela's management philosophy

jokes.text: I have no characteristics and people forget me as soon as I leave the room. And sometimes before that.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from forgettable to forgotten while present
jokes.explanation: Excellent character introduction - perfectly captures Frank's tragic invisibility with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: That's ridiculous! Um...
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately forgetting his name mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate validation of Frank's claim - proves his point through dramatic irony

jokes.text: No, that's an empty space between three stacks of boxes.
jokes.setup: Frank asking if empty space is his cubicle
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact description of non-workspace
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - emphasizes Frank's low status through workplace conditions

jokes.text: I don't have time for that. Temping is a full-time job.
jokes.setup: Suggestion Frank could travel for 5 cents
jokes.punchline: Frank's misguided work ethic about temporary employment
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Frank takes temporary work more seriously than permanent employees

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, I wanna say Dave? Frank. I'm the temp. I've been here two weeks.
jokes.setup: Guest trying to remember Frank's name
jokes.punchline: Wrong guess followed by Frank's patient correction and tenure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's invisibility - even after two weeks, still not remembered

jokes.text: I can't stand his toxic positivity. He likes everyone, even me! It's pathetic.
jokes.punchline: Bender finding kindness toward him personally offensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - being liked is somehow an insult to his awfulness

jokes.text: Wow. Fry's not even here and you remember his name? He must be quite a guy.
jokes.setup: Frank being constantly forgotten vs. absent Fry being remembered
jokes.punchline: Frank's impressed reaction to basic name retention
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Frank's amazement at normal human memory highlights his tragic situation

jokes.text: Who the hell is he?
jokes.setup: Frank working there for two weeks
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg still has no idea who he is
jokes.explanation: Good callback - even Zoidberg, usually oblivious, actively doesn't know Frank

jokes.text: A novelization of the TikTok video 'Peanut Butter Eating Dog Lip Syncs to Olivia Rodrigo.'
jokes.setup: Last book checked out from library
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific modern reference turned into literature
jokes.explanation: Good satire of modern content being treated as literature - specific enough to be funny

jokes.text: Ooh! I'd watch that. It's a book. You'd read it. No, I wouldn't.
jokes.punchline: Leela's correction and Amy's immediate rejection of reading
jokes.explanation: Good quick exchange showing future attitude toward books vs. visual media

jokes.text: Certainly not! It's been illegal to dump hazardous waste in the ocean for over a year now.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to dump books in ocean
jokes.punchline: Books classified as hazardous waste, recent law change
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - books as environmental hazard, law change implies recent dumping

jokes.text: Ew! I got book on me!
jokes.punchline: Treating book contact as contamination
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy - treating books like toxic waste continues the theme

jokes.text: So weird that reading used to be a thing.
jokes.punchline: Amy treating literacy as historical curiosity
jokes.explanation: Good dystopian observation - casual dismissal of reading as obsolete practice

jokes.text: Let's get outta here before some dork says trashing books is a travesty.
jokes.punchline: Bender preemptively mocking reasonable objection to destroying books
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bender dismissing intellectual concerns as dorky

jokes.text: Only a delivery to book hell. We're just lucky we made it back without reading anything.
jokes.punchline: Reading treated as dangerous contamination to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of anti-literacy theme - reading as hazard to survive

jokes.text: We did all make it back, right? / Well, let's see. There's me, Bender, and some other people... We're good.
jokes.setup: Leela asking if everyone made it back
jokes.punchline: Professor only counting himself and Bender as important
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific dismissiveness that sets up Frank being forgotten.

jokes.text: Five, 6, 7, 8. All eight crew members present and accounted for.
jokes.punchline: Counting implies someone missing but declaring all present
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Frank being forgotten - count suggests missing person but conclusion denies it

jokes.text: I can get through this. I'm a temp, dammit. I'm sure they'll be back soon.
jokes.punchline: Optimistic temp work ethic applied to abandonment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Frank's professional dedication even in abandonment is tragic and funny

jokes.text: When's the last time you took one? Twenty-three years ago, after that horrific snu-snu incident.
jokes.punchline: Callback to traumatic sexual experience from earlier episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback to classic episode - connects timeline and references traumatic comedy

jokes.text: What's-his-name! He had a name? Even I looked down on him.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from forgetting name to Bender's surprised condescension
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - even Bender, who's terrible to everyone, is surprised he looked down on Frank

jokes.text: We'll just swing by on the way to the wedding and pick up his corpse.
jokes.setup: Realizing they abandoned Frank for 23 years
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption he's dead, treating body retrieval as errand
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - treating corpse retrieval as casual inconvenience shows callousness

jokes.text: I thought you'd forgotten about me. No. We could never forget... Frank! Of course! Sounds right.
jokes.punchline: Crew still can't remember his name, awkward delayed recognition
jokes.explanation: Good callback - even at emotional reunion, they still can't remember his name naturally

jokes.text: Books! Book sandwiches, book soup, scrambled books with fly larvae! Ew, books!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate book-based cuisine, Amy's disgusted reaction to books not larvae
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect punchline - Amy disgusted by books, not the maggots

jokes.text: It's just as itchy as I dreamed it would be.
jokes.setup: Frank putting on clean clothes after 23 years
jokes.punchline: His long-held dream was about fabric texture discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Frank's simple pleasures after deprivation, focus on mundane discomfort

jokes.text: Yes, I am. I'm right here...
jokes.setup: Crew waiting for Fry to return
jokes.punchline: Frank's weak protest about being present and ignored
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment - Frank's pathetic attempt to assert his presence

jokes.text: Finally, a use for the Bible.
jokes.setup: Using books for some practical purpose
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive comment about holy text having utility
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender irreverence - consistent character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Why hast thou forsaken me?!
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference to abandonment, treating crew as deity
jokes.explanation: Good biblical reference - Frank's dramatic abandonment plea with religious language

jokes.text: She fell and broke a few of her elbows.
jokes.punchline: Multiple elbows injury implies alien anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good alien anatomy joke - casual mention of multiple elbows as mundane injury

jokes.text: I broke another one.
jokes.punchline: Immediate additional injury during conversation
jokes.explanation: Simple callback payoff - grandmother's continued clumsiness with timing

jokes.text: While during the same period, we're using twice as much soap in the men's room. I have noticed less body odor. Definitely. Way less.
jokes.punchline: Clue is improved hygiene, crew's relieved agreement
jokes.explanation: Good mystery setup - hygiene improvement as clue to identity switch

jokes.text: You're not Fry. That's Fry! Yes... Fry...
jokes.setup: Real Fry trying to prove identity
jokes.punchline: Frank's robotic confirmation of false identity
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic moment - Frank's mind control revealed through stilted speech

jokes.text: That could be me with anyone stupid. I have a picture with everybody!
jokes.setup: Frank showing photo evidence of being Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissal escalates to absurd claim about universal photography
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - dismisses evidence through increasingly absurd claims

jokes.text: And The Return of the King? That one I didn't read. It's the third book of the trilogy, and I've never read the first two. I didn't wanna spoil it, you know?
jokes.punchline: Refusing to read finale without prerequisites despite isolation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Frank's adherence to reading etiquette despite desperate circumstances is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Because you have such a great life! I've had so, so many temp jobs, but working at Planet Express was the one time in my life I was truly happy.
jokes.punchline: Fry's objectively terrible job was Frank's peak happiness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Frank's tragic perspective makes Fry's awful job seem enviable

jokes.text: Whoa. Bleak.
jokes.punchline: Fry's understated reaction to profound sadness
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - Fry's casual reaction to Frank's existential crisis

jokes.text: I'm already in my pajamas. No, I'm not.
jokes.setup: Bender making excuse not to help
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-correction revealing the lie
jokes.explanation: Great Bender moment - caught in lazy lie, accidentally tells truth

jokes.text: That's not a plan! You're just improvising!
jokes.punchline: Leela's critique of his villainous methodology
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor - Leela critiquing Frank's villainous planning like a writing instructor

jokes.text: How much radiation? Not much. Like having an affair with Marie Curie.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific historical/sexual reference for comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy - combines scientific reference with inappropriate personal comparison

jokes.text: I... think I was gonna throw it at somebody. Who? I can't remember.
jokes.punchline: Mind control makes him forget his purpose mid-action
jokes.explanation: Good resolution beat - shows mind control's effect on memory and purpose

jokes.text: Hey, why is the ship sideways? Who knows? It just falls over sometimes.
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of frequent mechanical failures
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist beat - treating major mechanical failure as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: I don't need a vacation. I just had six weeks off.
jokes.punchline: Fry thinking his replacement period was his vacation
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Fry's false memories of time under mind control as restful
